chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C. P. No. D-2777 of 2025 _______________________________________________________________________________________________ Date Order with Signature(s) of Judge(s) HEARING. 1. For hearing of Misc. No.12571/2025 (Stay) 2. For hearing of main case. 10.07.2025. Mr. Umer Sikander, Advocate for the Petitioner. Mr. K. V. Vaswami, AAG. ________________ We have heard learned counsel for the petitioner as well as learned Advocate General Sindh. It appears that the relief sought is almost the same as was given vide order dated 03.03.2025 in C.P. No.D-850 of 2025. Learned A.G. Sindh has no objection to similar relief being granted to the petitioner by further submitting that against a similar nature in similar terms Petition bearing C.P. No.D-2459/2025 was also decided by this Court vide Order dated 12.06.2025. Accordingly based on the same circumstances, this petition is disposed of alongwith listed application in terms of the said order which is reproduced below for ease of reference: “1. Granted. 2-4. Learned AAG is present and waives notice. It is jointly submitted that the present petition pertains to the vires of imposition of Infrastructure Cess. It is added that the matter has already been determined by a Division Bench of this Court in the case of Ibrahim Fibres Ltd. vs. Province of Sindh and others (C.P.D 3309 of 2011 judgment dated 04.06.2021). Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that the ratio of the aforesaid judgment is squarely applicable to the present facts and circumstances, hence, this petition may be dismissed in mutatis mutandis application thereof, leaving the petitioner at liberty to seek appellate remedy along with other similarly placed parties.

Province of Sindh and others (C.P.D 3309 of 2011 judgment dated 04.06.2021). Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that the ratio of the aforesaid judgment is squarely applicable to the present facts and circumstances, hence, this petition may be dismissed in mutatis mutandis application thereof, leaving the petitioner at liberty to seek appellate remedy along with other similarly placed parties. Learned AAG submits that he has no objection to the dismissal of the petition in this manner as aforesaid. Order accordingly. The instant petition is dismissed.” JUDGE JUDGE Nadeem
